req: REQ-024 (Project Blockers (operational health, independent of lifecycle))

issues.detail: “Types (v1)” defines a closed catalog, but no test rejects an unknown type; an implementation accepting arbitrary type strings could pass.
issues.fix: Attempt to raise a sentinel unknown type and assert rejection with no blocker or count change; custom cases must use the catch-all type.

issues.detail: “External dependency carries its dependency context” invents an undefined field/behavior. REQ-024 only names external dependency as a type; ordinary title/body coverage is already in AT-024.01.
issues.fix: Assert that an external-dependency blocker can be raised under the general field rules, without requiring an unspecified context field.

issues.detail: The test says repeated raising conditions upgrade severity, but the requirement permits an in-place upgrade only “when the condition worsens.” Repeating an unchanged condition must not itself escalate severity.
issues.fix: Separate unchanged-repeat and worsened-condition fixtures: unchanged repeats preserve id and severity; worsening updates severity on the same id.

issues.detail: The singleton assertion lacks a two-project fixture, so a wrongly global singleton could pass despite the required “per project” scope.
issues.fix: Trigger the same auto-raised type on two projects and assert one unresolved instance exists on each.

issues.detail: “Manual types” and “several are raised” could use different types, which would not prove that multiple instances of the same manual type may remain open.
issues.fix: Raise two blockers of the same manual type on one project and assert both distinct instances remain open.

issues.detail: Scoping AT-024.14 to non-Lovable-setup blockers removed coverage of the general “project flagged at-risk” consequence for a Lovable-setup blocker that reaches its eligible 7-day mutual-silence escalation. AT-024.15 checks admin involvement but not at-risk state.
issues.fix: In AT-024.15’s 7-day mutual-silence fixture, also assert that the project becomes at-risk.

issues.detail: The test asserts prominence after 48h but has no discriminating pre-threshold assertion; an implementation making every blocker prominent immediately could pass.
issues.fix: Use controlled 47h59m and post-48h fixtures and assert added prominence only on the latter.

issues.detail: “Its ABSENCE … means zero open blockers” mandates a zero-state representation not stated by REQ-024 or decision d71. The requirement requires current count/severity, but does not choose between an absent chip and an explicit zero chip.
issues.fix: Assert only that the showcase exposes no nonzero blocker signal at zero, or obtain a product ruling before prescribing chip absence.

issues.detail: The raise-path lifecycle assertion overlaps AT-005.5.38 despite the boundary note assigning lifecycle-state independence there. AT-024.24 adds only the resolution half.
issues.fix: Remove the duplicated raise half and retain a resolution-only independence test, or expand AT-005.5.38 to cover resolution and retire AT-024.24.

issues.detail: The fixture names an “awaiting-volunteer” blocker, which is not a v1 blocker type. An automated loop could implement this invented enum value.
issues.fix: Use a defined volunteer-tied fixture, such as the manual catch-all with action-owner set to the departing volunteer.

issues.detail: A single “abandonment release” fixture does not discriminate the two REQ-005.5 return-to-open paths: inactivity timeout and manual release. Cleanup could be wired to only one and still pass.
issues.fix: Parameterize release cleanup over both the 21-day timeout path and the manual-release path.
